sections.label: Hook
sections.text: Ketohexokinase is usually framed as the fructose enzyme that fuels fatty liver. But this paper adds a twist: under nutrient stress, KHK may also help liver cells survive.
sections.speaker_directions: Start with a measured, surprising tone. Pause briefly after 'twist'.

enhancement_cues.description: Bold title card: 'KHK: one enzyme, two jobs' with fructose molecule icon and hepatocyte outline.
Fructose and palmitate icons drift into a simplified liver cell.

sections.label: Setup
sections.text: In hepatocytes exposed to fructose plus saturated fat, the endoplasmic reticulum gets overloaded. Misfolded proteins pile up, and the cell has to choose: adapt, or trigger death programs.
sections.speaker_directions: Clear technical delivery; slow slightly on 'endoplasmic reticulum'.

enhancement_cues.description: Clean ER-stress diagram: nutrient overload → ER folding backlog → decision fork.
Key term card: 'ER proteostasis = keeping protein folding under control'.

sections.label: Build
sections.text: Here is the surprise. When KHK was present, stress signaling leaned toward the adaptive IRE-one-alpha and XBP-one pathway. When KHK was knocked down, protein aggregates and apoptotic signals rose.
sections.speaker_directions: Emphasize 'surprise'. Keep acronyms crisp and unhurried.

enhancement_cues.description: Split screen: left shows KHK present → blue adaptive IRE1α/XBP1 branch; right shows KHK loss → amber/red aggregate and apoptosis branch.
Display contrast card: 'KHK present: adaptive UPR' vs 'KHK loss: proteotoxic stress'.

sections.label: Climax
sections.text: So KHK is not just a metabolic accelerator. In this model, it is part of a stress-adaptation system that helps preserve ER proteostasis before the cell tips into apoptosis.
sections.speaker_directions: Deliver as the aha moment; warmer and slightly slower.

enhancement_cues.description: Pathway resolves into a central message: metabolism and proteostasis intersect at KHK.

sections.label: Landing
sections.text: That creates a therapeutic nuance: blocking harmful fructose flux may not be the same as deleting KHK entirely. The next question is how to separate enzyme activity from this protective stress role.
sections.speaker_directions: Conclude with thoughtful precision; leave room after the final sentence.

enhancement_cues.description: Therapeutic balance scale: reduce fructose flux on one side, preserve ER protection on the other.
CTA lower third: 'Read the article: doi.org/10.1152/ajpgi.00235.2025'.

metadata.review_summary: PASS: 0 critical findings; timing, narrative arc, and cue density meet script-stage criteria.

notes: Enhancement cues appear at least every 8-10 seconds.
Word count is within the 130-150 word budget for a 60 second technical explainer.
